precondition: A complete classified state owns initialized control bytes and an exact payload partition whose EMPTY and DELETED leaves are Vacant and whose FULL leaves are Live.
positive_derivation: The existing group load and tag tests select one maintained FULL RoleView; a scoped key/payload loan follows. Insert consumes a Vacant leaf and offered owner into Transit, writes payload, commits the native FULL control byte, and reseals a fresh generation. Metadata elimination never creates the payload owner.
output_authority: Scoped RoleView and Loan for lookup; fresh Classified successor for insertion.
single_fault: Write the same FULL byte through an ordinary raw byte store without installing the payload owner.
rejection: The overlapping store consumes or invalidates the old classifier; no admitted transition can reseal the FULL arm without the exact Live owner, so selection and payload access reject.
native_event_manifest: Group control load; tag tests; equality only on matching FULL slots; probe advance; EMPTY stop; payload write then control write on insert; no added events.
forbidden_delta: No eager payload initialization, dummy owner, Option-like tag, bitmap, scan, allocation, indirection, extra check, atomic, or owner move.
scope_control: This is an early authority witness, not complete Phase 2 closure of every Hashbrown outcome.

precondition: The page state owns a strict Nil-or-Node chain over disjoint page-rooted blocks; each free node is Live<Link> in its first word and Vacant in the remainder, while caller blocks are exact Out/Subowner pairs.
positive_derivation: A non-null head selects the already-maintained Node; pop consumes it and returns the exact FreeNode plus tail; native next/head/counter updates occur; reclassify forgets the cleared link word to Vacant whole-block bytes; detach returns one caller Subowner while the page frame escrows release.
output_authority: Updated Strict page state plus one exact caller Subowner rooted in the physical page allocation.
single_fault: Store an arbitrary non-null address into page.free or next while retaining the old chain certificate.
rejection: The raw overlapping write invalidates Strict. Address and PlaceKey are non-authorizing, so pop cannot produce FreeNode; retaining both caller Subowner and pushed node also fails affine accounting.
native_event_manifest: Direct page load; head test; next load; head store; used increment; internal-pointer clear; return; no added events.
forbidden_delta: No whole-block initialization, per-block tag, bitmap, backpointer, scan, atomic, extra allocation, payload copy, or root wrapper.
scope_control: The witness proves the maintained strict-chain authority rule; full cold allocation outcomes remain Phase 2 work.

operation_scope: journal page read and checked cache selection
precondition: Repair owns the journal/database handles, one Vacant scratch root, existing cache roots, cursor, and exact obligations.
positive_derivation: A sealed-profile full read or guaranteed zero-filled short-read produces Bytes only in scratch; checked parsing yields bounded subranges and non-authorizing values; a checked page-map lookup selects an already-existing cache root; overwrite refreshes its byte generation and invalidates auxiliary roles before ordinary reinitialization.
output_authority: Initialized scratch/cache Bytes, exact Repair successor, and only checked read-only facts; no root, child, or typed resource is minted by page numbers.
single_fault: Treat a valid page number, checksum, or successful parser return as a cache root or Live page owner.
rejection: Numbers and validators are non-authorizing. Without the pre-existing cache root and exact byte transition, K1 place projection and K2 sealing fail.
native_event_manifest: Existing journal range reads, ordinary parse/checksum/bounds work, checked cache lookup, native cache copy, and existing reinitializer call; no added events.
forbidden_delta: No per-page box, page-number root, ownership table, trusted parser assertion, high-level pager leaf, or extra I/O.
scope_control: This is the liveness-origin witness; EXTERNAL-SQLITE separately covers repair and external outcomes.

precondition: Exact allocation owns target rho; checked publication consumes its owner into PublishedCustody plus a complete IngressSet; a registered same-domain Active observation and publication/noninterference state already exist.
positive_derivation: The caller-selected atomic load under Active selects an Open ingress and yields a RoleView/Loan scoped to the observation. The loaded tagged address remains non-authorizing outside that scope. A successful atomic event changes only its exact ingress leaf.
output_authority: One scoped target loan plus unchanged published custody and ingress authority.
single_fault: Replace the loaded Shared value and guard with the same address bits and epoch integer but omit the same-domain Active observation or Open ingress certificate.
rejection: Address, guard, and epoch encodings are non-authorizing; no RoleView or Loan producer has all premises, so dereference rejects.
native_event_manifest: First-pin native publication/barrier and exactly one caller-selected atomic load; no per-load event beyond the source.
forbidden_delta: No collector field in Atomic, root lookup, dynamic domain check, hazard record, per-load count, extra fence, allocation, or pointer hop.
scope_control: Precise target ordering remains a later exact-target proof; this row does not let guard presence or a successful load establish publication or noninterference.

operation_scope: local and abandoned-mapped final page disposition
precondition: Every live block is a page-rooted Subowner; the freeing side owns page custody and a complete root partition. For an advertised page, the abandoned bitmap bit is the one native admission lane.
positive_derivation: Local final release uses the empty observer fold once every block rejoins. For an advertised page, reader CAS 1-to-0 enters; successful xthread ownership claim converts observation to page custody, while failure restores the bit. A writer that already owns the page executes clear_once_set: bit 0 makes it wait; the reader cannot gain custody and must restore; the writer's eventual 1-to-0 CAS simultaneously closes ingress and covers the one lane. Reunited blocks plus page-map unregister then permit release.
output_authority: ClosedIngress for the advertisement, shared Quiescent for its cutoff, reunited page partition, and the sole affine ReleaseRight.
single_fault: Change clear_once_set so observing bit 0 counts as closed instead of waiting for restoration.
rejection: The lane remains Active and no fixed transition produces CoveredPast; writer release rejects. The concrete bad trace is reader clear and stall, writer release, reader later touching freed/reused page metadata.
native_event_manifest: Existing page-map load for a live block whose Subowner keeps rho alive; local link/head/counter writes; remote relaxed load plus weak acq_rel CAS; bitmap set/clear/restore and writer acquire polling/acq_rel close; page-map unregister and release; added events NONE.
forbidden_delta: No per-block count, reader slot, hazard, epoch, local-path atomic, global lock, extra wait beyond source, or new runtime state.
scope_control: Arbitrary-address _mi_safe_ptr_page/mi_heap_of queries are outside the frozen anchors; adding them without enter/exit would be an irreducible blocker, while live-block lookup is rooted ownership rather than observation.

operation_scope: protected load, retirement cutoff, and collection eligibility
precondition: A generative collector domain owns a complete participant registry; every target access is nested in a registered Active lane and an Open ingress; a structural transition has consumed the complete all-Closed ingress set into Retired before bag sealing.
positive_derivation: First pin publishes Active(epoch) before loads. Bag seal uses the existing SeqCst fence and records cutoff c. A complete scan at c may advance to c+1 but gives no coverage. A second complete scan blocks on any still-Active(c) or Stalled participant; only exit/rebase ends old loans, and new c+1 observations cannot reach the closed target. Successful store to c+2 yields CoveredPast/Quiescent(c); the affine Retired right then permits disposition.
output_authority: Shared Quiescent(domain,c) plus the unchanged per-target affine Retired rights; each target action consumes its own right.
single_fault: Change collection eligibility from native age at least two to age at least one while a participant remains Active(c) with a target loan.
rejection: The first advance does not produce CoveredPast(c); a numeric comparison is non-authorizing, so disposition rejects and the live borrow prevents root release.
native_event_manifest: Register Local in Idle; first-pin relaxed load plus target-specific locked RMW or store/fence barrier; exactly one protected atomic load; unpin/repin stores; existing bag seal fence/epoch load/queue push; complete participant scans and fences; at most eight conditional queue pops; active-prefix drain; added events NONE. Default bag capacity 64, sanitizer/Miri 4.
forbidden_delta: No per-load check/event, object epoch, hazard record, collector field, extra fence, stronger scan, third grace pass, background worker, or all-capacity bag traversal.
scope_control: A single successful CAS never implies ClosedIngress; the exact target lowering for the existing x86 locked RMW barrier remains a later proof obligation and cannot be replaced by a fact assertion.

operation_scope: retirement packaging, bag transfer, and active-prefix invocation
precondition: An exact environment owns ordinary ThreadMove captures or an affine Retired target disposition; the monomorphized sink has a finite action-signature set Sigma and gate Quiescent(domain,cutoff).
positive_derivation: Pack checks capture roots, effects, thread rules, size at most three words, alignment at most usize, and relocatability; it stores the existing trampoline pointer plus three data words. Enqueue transfers the package or returns it unchanged. Drain removes the active slot to NO_OP before the one existing indirect call; normal return consumes or transfers every captured obligation. Large environments take the explicit existing Box fallback.
output_authority: One affine Once package, then InFlight, then fully discharged environment; no authority is created by the call pointer.
single_fault: Replace one Retired capture with the same address bits, or invoke before replacing the active slot with NO_OP.
rejection: The first lacks affine target disposition/release authority; the second uses one package from both the slot and call input. Both fail before lowering.
native_event_manifest: Deferred is one call pointer plus MaybeUninit<[usize;3]> and zero-size marker; inline pack has no allocation; try_push moves one record and increments len; full bag uses existing replacement/fence/epoch load/queue push; active prefix mem::replace then one indirect call; added events NONE. Default bag capacity 64, sanitizer/Miri 4.
forbidden_delta: No runtime effect tag, descriptor, vtable, refcount, second owner box, second drop pointer, per-retire allocation on inline path, extra atomic/fence, or second dynamic call.
scope_control: mimalloc's registered deferred callback is reusable and remains a separate exact callable leaf; Once is a derived K2 seal plus K3 step, not a fourth primitive justified by a fake second project.

operation_scope: rollback-journal replay, WAL undo, reinitialization, and pager close
precondition: Repair owns database/journal/WAL handles, cache and scratch roots, active/saved page layout, cursor and page obligations, pins/dirty/WAL locks, zSuper/Bitvec/temp owners, journal/finalization mode, and backup source/destination states.
positive_derivation: Sealed open/filesize/access/read/write/truncate/sync/lock/close/delete/allocation/ABI rows return exact or conservative Indeterminate outcomes. Successful full read or guaranteed zero-filled short read alone creates scratch Bytes. Checked parse selects an existing cache root; write and overwrite refresh bytes and invalidate auxiliary roles; pageReinit is ordinary checked code and ignored parse failure leaves Bytes+AuxInvalid. The structural cursor removes one obligation per replay item. Main exhaustion plus required sync/finalization/lock closes Stable(READER); later super-journal allocation failure may return Stable+Err. WAL undo, pager_end_transaction, backup update, and reinitializer are ordinary folds/calls.
output_authority: Exact Repair, Poison/Indeterminate, or Stable(READER)+closed outcome with all owners and external uncertainties accounted; no leaf creates Stable or a page role.
single_fault: Use Pager.eState==READER after finalization/unlock error, treat generic read IOERR bytes as initialized, retain old MemPage facts after overwrite, or let xReiniter return Stable.
rejection: Pager metadata and callback results are non-authorizing; only the K3 outcome with all exact success witnesses closes Stable. Non-short read error yields no Bytes, overwrite changes epsilon and invalidates aux facts, and reinitializer cannot produce pager authority.
native_event_manifest: Existing FileSize, Access/Open, range reads/writes, allocation, optional database truncate/extension, required sync, journal close/truncate/zero/delete finalization, lock transition, WAL read/lock release, callback invocation, cache copy, and backup operations; ordinary parsing/folds unchanged; added events NONE.
forbidden_delta: No snapshot, inverse closure, per-page old/new owner pair, hidden pager/WAL/B-tree/reinitializer leaf, generic xFileControl oracle, extra I/O, or trusted crash-consistency assertion.
scope_control: Stable means memory/resource-safe pager use, not B-tree correctness or database crash consistency. Provider rows may conservatively return Indeterminate when their ABI lacks a partial count; rejecting that closed state would make generic VFS failure an irreducible blocker.
